codeReview.summary: The feature adds real-Postgres lifecycle coverage and a thin idle-current transport smoke, but it still misses one documented invalid-input case and duplicates an existing idle-current application assertion.

issues.description: VAL-ENTRY-003 requires rejecting both `stop < start` and `start + duration != stop`. The committed suite only covered the stop-before-start path; the mismatched `start + duration != stop` case described in the feature and milestone contract was not part of commit `e8d0ccd`, so the feature did not fully satisfy the invalid time-range rejection requirement.

`TestGetCurrentTimeEntryReturnsNullWhenIdle` duplicates the pre-existing `TestGetCurrentTimeEntry_ReturnsEmptyViewWhenNoRunningEntry` instead of extending the existing test surface, which goes against the repo's reuse-first guidance and adds maintenance churn without new signal.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-test-worker skill correctly says to convert fulfilled assertions into explicit acceptance cases, but this worker still ended the feature without covering the full documented invalid-range matrix for VAL-ENTRY-003. The skill may need a stronger reminder to enumerate every acceptance bullet from `expectedBehavior` / `fulfills` before concluding coverage is complete.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature contract lists invalid `start`/`stop`/`duration` inputs broadly; commit `e8d0ccd` only added `TestInvalidTimeRangeIsRejectedWithoutMutation` for `stop < start` and the transcript handoff still claimed full VAL-ENTRY-003 coverage.

summary: Review failed: the feature's new backend tests are mostly aligned, but commit `e8d0ccd` did not actually cover the full invalid-time-range requirement because it omitted the `start + duration != stop` rejection case mandated by VAL-ENTRY-003.
